title: GoodNewsApp.tsx is a re-export shim for GoodNewsLegacyApp
summary: Entire GoodNewsApp.tsx is a 7-line re-export of GoodNewsLegacyApp. The only consumers are the catalog's dynamic import and the rollback test. The shim dates to a reverted broadsheet-wrapper refactor; its job is done.
proposed_action: Inline the import in catalog.tsx and the test to point directly at './goodnews/GoodNewsLegacyApp', then delete apps/rad-os/components/apps/GoodNewsApp.tsx.

title: Deprecated AppWindowBody / AppWindowSplitView / AppWindowPane exports have no runtime consumers
summary: All three are marked @deprecated. Full-repo grep confirms no runtime consumer in apps/rad-os or any other package uses AppWindowBody, AppWindowSplitView, AppWindowPane (or AppWindow.Body/SplitView/Pane). Only references are the component's own test file and generated meta/schema artifacts. The documented migration to AppWindow.Content + AppWindow.Island is complete.
proposed_action: Remove the deprecated functions, their compound-object aliases, the named exports in AppWindow.tsx, the export in components/core/index.ts, and the corresponding entries in AppWindow.meta.ts. Regenerate AppWindow.schema.json via pnpm registry:generate. Also regenerate generated/figma/contracts/app-window.contract.json.

title: Deprecated useTabsState hook is only referenced by its own backward-compat tests
summary: useTabsState is @deprecated and carries an explicit 'Backward compat' banner with legacy layout-to-position mapping. Repo-wide grep confirms the only references are the smoke and Tabs tests. The Tabs refactor (docs/plans/2026-03-30-tabs-coherent-refactor.md) is fully rolled out across RadOS, WindowTabs, BrandAssetsApp, RadiantsStudioApp, and runtime-attachments demos.
proposed_action: Remove the useTabsState export and TabsPosition layout map. Drop the export from packages/radiants/components/core/index.ts. Delete the backward-compat sub-describe in Tabs.test.tsx and the expectation in smoke.test.tsx.

title: Input.iconName prop is @deprecated and has no runtime callers
summary: iconName is @deprecated in favor of `icon`. Grep across the monorepo shows no call site passes iconName= to <Input>; all consumers use the icon prop. The component still carries the `hasIcon = Boolean(icon || iconName)` branch and a `data-icon-slot` placeholder path that nothing feeds.
proposed_action: Remove the iconName prop from InputProps, Input.meta.ts, and the InputControl render logic. Collapse hasIcon to Boolean(icon) and remove the data-icon-slot placeholder fallback. Regenerate Input.schema.json.

title: Icon demo in runtime-attachments still forwards removed iconSet prop
summary: The Icon demo destructures `iconSet` and conditionally spreads it onto <Icon iconSet={...}>. Icon.tsx no longer declares iconSet in IconProps, and the rdna/no-arbitrary-icon-size ESLint rule explicitly bans the prop. Nothing produces iconSet anywhere, so the conditional is dead.
proposed_action: Remove `iconSet` from the Icon demo's destructure and delete the conditional spread at line 363.

title: AppWindow titlebar portal-slot fallback has no consumers
summary: `{navContent || <div id={`window-titlebar-slot-${id}`} className='contents' />}` is the legacy-app fallback from the pre-navContent AppWindow API. No code in apps/ or packages/ calls createPortal against, or uses getElementById for, `window-titlebar-slot-...`. The brainstorm explicitly flagged this for eventual removal once migration finished.
proposed_action: Replace the branch with `{navContent}` and drop the comment. No other edits needed.

title: PropControls.getEnumValues has a dead legacyEnum fallback
summary: PropDef no longer has an `enum` field — only `values` and `options`. No *.meta.ts and no generated *.schema.json in the repo emits `enum:`. The `(prop as PropDef & { enum?: ... }).enum` cast and its `?.length` check are a dead compat branch from before the meta-first generator.
proposed_action: Remove the legacyEnum block and return `undefined` directly after options/values checks.

title: pretext/legacy.ts coerceStoredDoc has no runtime consumer (borderline)
summary: `coerceStoredDoc`, `ScratchpadDraft`, and related legacy/pretext discriminated-union types are imported only by pretext-serialization.test.ts. The active ScratchpadApp uses its own BlockNote-centric `use-scratchpad-docs.ts` migration (LEGACY_KEY = 'rados-scratchpad') and never calls coerceStoredDoc. This appears to be pre-staged scaffolding for a pretext→scratchpad merge that has not shipped.
proposed_action: Report-only. Defer to Agent 3 (dead code) if product-direction confirms the pretext-scratchpad integration is not planned; otherwise keep.

title: tokens.css backward-compat alias block — protected by guardrails
summary: Explicitly protected by the audit guardrails. Removing the aliases (--color-accent, --color-accent-inv, --color-accent-soft, --color-danger, --color-success, --color-warning) would break Tailwind v4 utility generation per user's documented token-chaining rules.

title: plugin.configs['recommended-strict'] is an intentional future migration target
summary: CLAUDE.md marks 'Not yet activated — migration target'. The test locks its shape. Removing it would destroy the planned strict-mode on-ramp.

title: PatternEntry.legacyName is still a runtime lookup key
summary: getPatternByName allows lookup by legacyName, and <Pattern pat='checker-32'> is asserted to resolve. This is an active public API path, not a dead shim.

title: LEGACY_ALIASES in pixel-corners.config.mjs generate the widely-consumed pixel-rounded t-shirt classes
summary: Despite the 'LEGACY' name, these aliases produce the pixel-rounded-xs/sm/md/lg/xl CSS classes that are consumed by 40+ RDNA components and rad-os apps. Actively load-bearing.
proposed_action: Do not remove. Consider renaming the variable later for clarity, but outside this agent's scope.

title: Persisted-state and localStorage migrations read legacy keys
summary: Standard versioned migrations that read and then delete old localStorage keys (rados-favorites, rados-scratchpad) for live users. Removing these would silently drop existing user data on upgrade.

title: Checkbox standalone-mode 'backwards compat' comment marks an active branch
summary: The comment describes a supported standalone API, not a legacy shim. The checked/onChange surface is documented and tested.
